content: For short-text BR transaction classification (merchant descriptor -> category), all three target providers are cheap enough that memory-first keeps cost near zero. Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite ~$0.10/$0.40 per M tokens with a free tier (CLAUDE.md HIGH source). Claude Haiku 4.5 (id claude-haiku-4-5) $1.00/$5.00 per M tokens, 200K context (Claude API skill, HIGH). DeepSeek: legacy deepseek-chat deprecates 2026-07-24, now routes to deepseek-v4-flash ($0.14 cache-miss in / $0.28 out per M); model id 'deepseek-chat' still works near-term. Any of these handle a one-shot batch of unseen descriptors per upload trivially. Differentiation is cost/free-tier and which key the user already holds, not classification quality at this task.
